response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software craftsmanship fundamentals and broad technical depth, they are a poor fit for this role. C++ expertise ended 10+ years ago (2014–2015); the resume shows a deliberate shift toward TypeScript, web infrastructure, and AI agents. Most critically, there is zero mention of industrial automation, cybersecurity standards, IEC 62443, industrial protocols, or secure development lifecycle practices—all core requirements. The candidate would require substantial ramp-up on both the technical stack (modern C++ systems programming) and the domain (industrial automation, compliance standards), making them uncompetitive against candidates with active expertise in these areas.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship foundation with proven TDD, CI/CD, and code-quality discipline across multiple tech stacks.
Demonstrated ability to learn diverse languages and technologies quickly, from C/C++ to DLang to TypeScript.
Active open-source contributor and mentor with workshops and teaching experience, indicating ability to share knowledge.
concerns: C++ expertise is 10+ years stale (last role 2014–2015); no evidence of recent low-level systems programming or modern C++ standards.
No industrial automation, industrial cybersecurity, IEC 62443 standards, or secure development lifecycle experience—all core to the role.
Current career trajectory shows active movement away from systems programming (now TypeScript/AI agents); significant re-skilling would be required.

key_factors.factor: C++ and systems programming expertise gap
Industrial automation and cybersecurity standards absence
